brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Y) with fully valid reasoning about substrate independence and domain-general replicator dynamics; performs multi-level analysis: conflation trap, genuine-rung test, structural predicate shift, and why linguistic complexity misleads; explicitly names the pseudo-abstraction trap and checks whether Y is genuine rung-climbing or word dilution before committing; applies an explicit boundary-condition test (names three instance classes Y covers that X structurally cannot) as verification; acknowledges the conflation assumption clearly but does not explicitly state confidence levels or enumerate what would falsify its own conclusion; systematically evaluates the 'simpler = more abstract' alternative and rejects it with specific reasons; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation with full step-by-step logic a third party could reproduce; explicitly avoids ungrounded abstraction by anchoring each claim to concrete referents (memes, computer viruses, autocatalytic reactions) and names pseudo-abstraction as the failure mode to suppress; avoids all listed suppressed failure modes — no floating abstraction, no referent evaporation, no level lock-in, no pseudo-abstraction — and explicitly flags the pseudo-abstraction failure mode; shows structured branching reasoning (trap identification → test definition → example testing → structural predicate → verdict) but does not closely map to the hop-count monitoring loop topology from the injection.
